page: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured with an x86_64 architecture. It features 32 physical Intel CPU cores with a shared allocation model and a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system is equipped with 375.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 11.72 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes two NVIDIA L40S GPUs built on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined total of 96 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the configuration bundles a 2200 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the high-density GPU and memory configuration, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive workloads that require substantial parallel processing power and large memory footprints. The combination of dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs, 375 GB of RAM, and fast local NVMe storage makes this instance suitable for machine learning training, large language model inference, data science pipelines, 3D rendering, and high-performance computing tasks.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU instance designed for high-performance parallel computing. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 physical Intel CPU cores with a shared allocation model and a single thread per core. The server is equipped with 375.0 GB of system memory, yielding 11.72 GB of RAM per core. Graphics processing is driven by two NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs, providing a combined 96 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a bundled 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This hardware configuration is optimized for workloads requiring substantial GPU acceleration and memory capacity, such as machine learning model training, large-scale AI inference, data science pipelines, and complex 3D rendering tasks.
